chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI. C.P No. D-847 of 2023 _____________________________________________________________ Dated Order with signature of Judge. _____________________________________________________________ Priority 1. For hearing of Misc. No.3918/2023 2. For hearing of main case. 23.02.2023. Mr. Imtiaz Ali Langah, Advocate for the Petitioner Kazi Abdul Hameed Siddiqui, D.A.G. Mr. Muhammad Wasiq Mirza, Advocate for the Respondent No.4 along with Syed Israr Hussain, Advocate Mr. Anwar Alam, officer incharge, PMDC office. --------------- YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, J.- The Petitioner apparently sat the MDCAT 2022-23 and was shown to have secured only 46 marks and accordingly declared to have failed. Her case is that upon personally rechecking and calculating her marks in comparison with the relevant answer key, it transpired that her marks were significantly greater in number, hence she raised such grievance through the relevant Objection Submitting Form. The comments submitted by Dow University of Health Sciences reflect that the Petitioner approached the University for resolution of her grievance and her matter was taken along with identical grievances raised by certain other students, with it coming to the fore upon scrutiny / review that her correct result was 151 instead of 46, as earlier announced. Learned counsel for the University submits, however, that the final merit list for the admission session 2022-23 in Public Sector College / University has already been prepared and issued, but concedes that the result remains valid for a period of two years, and the same could be availed during that period for the purpose of admission to a Private Medical College, or in the event that seats at any Public Sector Colleges otherwise fall vacant for any reason.

The comments submitted by Dow University of Health Sciences reflect that the Petitioner approached the University for resolution of her grievance and her matter was taken along with identical grievances raised by certain other students, with it coming to the fore upon scrutiny / review that her correct result was 151 instead of 46, as earlier announced. Learned counsel for the University submits, however, that the final merit list for the admission session 2022-23 in Public Sector College / University has already been prepared and issued, but concedes that the result remains valid for a period of two years, and the same could be availed during that period for the purpose of admission to a Private Medical College, or in the event that seats at any Public Sector Colleges otherwise fall vacant for any reason. That being so, we allow the Petition to the extent of prayer Clause –A by directing the Respondent Nos.3 & 4 so as to update and duly reflect the revised result of the Petitioner as aforementioned. The Petition stands disposed of in such terms. JUDGE CHIEF JUSTICE TariqAli/PA
